metadata.title: States PCS: Assam GK Questions for Assam Public Service Commission Exams

questions.question: Which railway line links upper Assam and Arunachal Pradesh connecting Chaulkhowa and Moranhat to Sisibargaon?
questions.explanation: The Rangia-Murkongselek Railway connects upper Assam with Arunachal Pradesh, linking South Bank stations Chaulkhowa and Moranhat to North Bank stations Sisibargaon and Siripani. It serves as a major rail route facilitating transportation in this region of northeastern India.

questions.question: In which Assam district is the commercial town Jonai Bazar located?
questions.explanation: Jonai Bazar is in Dhemaji district of Assam. Dhemaji district was created in 1989 when it was carved out of Lakhimpur district. Jonai is one of the main commercial towns in Dhemaji, approximately 100 km from the district headquarters. The area is known for its trade and local markets within this district.

questions.question: Which Assam district has wetlands like Gaurisagar and Joysagar Pukhuri?
questions.explanation: Sivasagar district in Assam contains the Gaurisagar wetland and Joysagar Pukhuri. These water bodies are historically and ecologically significant. Joysagar Pukhuri was constructed in 1697 during the reign of Ahom king Rudra Singha. The district supports biodiversity and local fisheries through these wetlands.

questions.question: What is the traditional title of Chiefs in the Khasi tribes of Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: The Khasi tribes of Meghalaya have petty states called hima, each led by a chief titled Syiem. This title is traditional and specific to Khasi chiefs. The Khasi Hills region includes East and West Khasi Hills districts in Meghalaya. The term Syiem distinguishes Khasi chiefs from other administrative titles used elsewhere.

questions.question: What is the title of the local leader in Assamese villages?
questions.explanation: A Gaonburha is the appointed leader of a village (gaon) in Assam. The position holds authority mainly in rural villages, distinct from Mouzadars who manage administrative units called mouzas. Gaonburhas often handle local disputes and village administration. Their role is traditional and recognized in Assamese rural governance structures.

questions.question: Which tribe introduced ancient sericulture producing Pat silk in Assam?
questions.explanation: Pat silk is a mulberry silk from Assam, known for its durability and glossy texture. It is produced by larvae feeding on Morus alba leaves. The ancient practice of sericulture producing Pat silk was introduced by the Tibeto-Burman Kachari tribe in Assam, which contributed to the region’s silk industry development.

questions.question: What is the fermented bamboo shoot pickle in Assamese cuisine?
questions.explanation: Khorisa is a fermented bamboo shoot pickle traditionally used in Assamese cuisine. It involves natural fermentation of bamboo shoots, commonly consumed in Assam. Assamese cuisine often features fermented products, including khorisa for its unique flavor and preservation method. Other options like Gundruk and Kinema are fermented foods from different regions in India, not Assam.

questions.question: Which traditional Assamese ornament resembles a conical hat?
questions.explanation: Japi is a traditional Assamese ornament designed in the shape of a conical hat. It originates from Assam and is often used as a decorative and functional headgear. The Japi is crafted from bamboo and tokou leaves and has cultural significance in Assamese society.

questions.question: In Assam’s Eka-Sarana-Nama-Dharma, what is the title of a Satra’s head?
questions.explanation: The head of a Satra in Assam’s Eka-Sarana-Nama-Dharma tradition is called the Satradhikar. This person holds spiritual and administrative authority within the Satra institution.

questions.question: Who was awarded the title ‘Natasurya’ for contributions to Assamese drama?
questions.explanation: Phani Sharma acted in Assam’s first film Joymati (1935) and received the title ‘Natasurya’ for his impact on Assamese drama.
